title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Exploring the vast (0) DEPTHS of our oceans reveals a world that remains largely (17) to science. For centuries, humans were (18) of reaching the seafloor due to the extreme (19) exerted by miles of water. However, recent technological (20) have allowed us to send remote vehicles into the abyss. Each mission brings a startling (21) about the creatures living there. These organisms have developed unique (22) to survive in total darkness and freezing temperatures. Their (23) depends on nutrients falling from the surface or chemical energy from vents. Scientists have studied these environments (24) to understand the origins of life on Earth. Despite these efforts, much of the ocean floor remains unmapped. Protecting these fragile ecosystems is vital for the planet’s health, as they play a crucial role in regulating our climate.

example.text: Exploring the vast (0) DEPTHS (DEPTH) of our oceans reveals a world...
example.explanation: Plural noun required to denote deep areas; DEPTH → DEPTHS.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to describe the 'world'; MYSTERY → MYSTERIOUS.
Adjective with a negative prefix required to show inability; CAPABLE → INCAPABLE.
Plural noun required to describe biological changes; ADAPT → ADAPTATIONS.
Noun required as the subject of the sentence; SURVIVE → SURVIVAL.
Adverb required to modify the verb 'studied'; EXTEND → EXTENSIVELY.
